title: Validate contextdir on build
body:
We never verified that the context directory passed into the build
command was a valid directory.  When we then slapped a default Containerfile
name onto it, things went south fast if the user had passed us a file and
not a directory.

Fixes: #4383

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Replaces #4388.   @vrothberg there are no build tests in Podman.  Long story short, it wasn't possible to do once upon a time so we relied on Buildah side.  However, I think given the changes to the test systems that @baude has done, we could do build tests here now.  I didn't include any with this PR, but promise to start trying to add some in the near future, especially in the corner cases where podman build works differently than buildah bud.

Testing:
```
# podman build file
Error: context must be a directory: /tmp/file

# docker build file
unable to prepare context: context must be a directory: /tmp/file
```
@TomSweeneyRedHat Could you add the tests, to make sure we don't get a regression.
@rhatdan I'm not sure I'll get to the tests before early next week, but promise to take a look.  Can we push this through now to cure the issue and I'll pinky swear to the tests?
All green now, can we push this through?

:pushpin: Commit 677a0e5 has been approved by `vrothberg`
